stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Helen Oyeyemi is a British author.
  • In 2014, she published Boy, Snow, Bird, a novel of twelve interlocking chapters.
  • The chapters reframe classic fairy-tale motifs through the experiences of three women in a New England town.
  • In 2019, she published Gingerbread, a novel of nine interlocking chapters.
  • The chapters reframe classic fairy-tale motifs through the experiences of a family in a London household.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two books by Helen Oyeyemi. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Helen Oyeyemi’s novels Boy, Snow, Bird and Gingerbread both reframe classic fairy-tale motifs through the experiences of their characters.
Helen Oyeyemi’s Boy, Snow, Bird, which reframes classic fairy-tale motifs, contains more interlocking chapters than Gingerbread does.
Boy, Snow, Bird was published in 2014, and Gingerbread was published in 2019.
Unlike Gingerbread, Helen Oyeyemi’s novel Boy, Snow, Bird is set in a New England town.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. It names both novels and uses “both” to assert a shared feature: each reframes classic fairy-tale motifs through its characters’ experiences.

Choice B states a difference (chapter count). Choice C reports different publication years. Choice D explicitly contrasts the two novels.
